questions.question: Who is interviewed as the author of *Disillusioned*?
Where does Josh Shuping pastor, as stated in the interview?
Which three theological areas does Shuping treat as central in his book?
options: Sola scriptura, sola fide, and sola gratia only
Essence–energies, the filioque, and papal primacy only

questions.question: How does Shuping characterize Eastern Orthodox exclusivistic ecclesiology in this conversation?
options: As claiming that outside their formal communion others are not truly Christian or saved
As teaching that all Protestants are in full communion without baptism
As denying that bishops have any role in discipline
As identical to Cyprian’s rigorist movement in every respect
questions.answer: As claiming that outside their formal communion others are not truly Christian or saved

questions.question: Which synod does the interview cite for stark claims about being under an Eastern Orthodox bishop?
Which theologian is mentioned in connection with teaching at Princeton after Westminster professors had left for Westminster Seminary?
What does Ortlund clarify about his use of Matthew 7 (good tree, good fruit)?
options: He rejects institutional exclusivism he sees as unbiblical, not advocating universalism or pluralism
He teaches that any peaceful religion proves salvation
He argues that visible church membership is irrelevant
He denies that confession of Christ matters for discerning true faith
questions.answer: He rejects institutional exclusivism he sees as unbiblical, not advocating universalism or pluralism

questions.question: Which passage do the speakers use as a doctrinal test alongside the fruit of a life (e.g., contrast with Buddhism)?
From which page of Shuping’s book does Ortlund read the quote about a man-centered hunt for the “one true church”?
What pastoral move does Shuping describe for people paralyzed by ecclesial anxiety?
options: Directing them to master contemporary patriarchal politics first
Telling them to ignore the creeds
Taking them to the Nicene or Apostles’ Creed and treating the church as an object of faith trusted in Christ, not mere calculation
Advising them to join whichever parish has the oldest building
questions.answer: Taking them to the Nicene or Apostles’ Creed and treating the church as an object of faith trusted in Christ, not mere calculation

questions.question: Which documents does Shuping cite as examples of confessional Protestantism that do not claim “we alone are the church” in the way he criticizes?
options: Book of Concord, Westminster Standards, Three Forms of Unity, Thirty-Nine Articles, and 1689 London Baptist Confession
Only the canons of Nicaea II
Only the documents of Vatican II
questions.answer: Book of Concord, Westminster Standards, Three Forms of Unity, Thirty-Nine Articles, and 1689 London Baptist Confession

questions.question: How does Shuping describe early Christian apologists’ witness regarding image veneration?
options: As silent on the topic so nothing can be inferred
As uniformly celebrating mandatory icon kisses by the second century
As repeatedly indicating Christians did not venerate images—an *a fortiori* style argument, not a mere argument from silence
As endorsing the distinction between latria and dulia in systematic form
questions.answer: As repeatedly indicating Christians did not venerate images—an *a fortiori* style argument, not a mere argument from silence

questions.question: Which council’s acts and anathemas are central to Shuping’s canonical argument about icons?
Whose scholarly work on Nicaea II does Ortlund cite as supporting the iconoclast historical narrative without Protestant bias?
What biblical issue does Shuping emphasize alongside the Second Commandment in critiquing mandatory image veneration?
options: That the Old Testament never mentions kings
That if incarnation changed the commandment, one would expect explicit New Testament clarification
That the Gospels deny the resurrection
questions.answer: That if incarnation changed the commandment, one would expect explicit New Testament clarification

questions.question: What contrast does Shuping draw regarding “free” icon piety at the parish level versus conciliar teaching?
options: He says the council requires forced affection/veneration, not merely optional local custom
He says the council abolished all icons
He says parish priests always deny icons exist
He says Nicaea II was purely about church architecture permits
questions.answer: He says the council requires forced affection/veneration, not merely optional local custom

questions.question: Which Marian devotion does Shuping say aligns with the strong intercessory language in the prayers Ortlund reads?
How does Shuping describe the placement of Marian feasts in the liturgical year (as summarized in the interview)?
options: Mary has no feasts in Orthodoxy
Only Christmas and Easter mention Mary
Roughly half of the major yearly feasts concern Mary, framing the year (e.g., Nativity of Mary early, Dormition late)
All feasts are exclusively about the Trinity with no Marian themes
questions.answer: Roughly half of the major yearly feasts concern Mary, framing the year (e.g., Nativity of Mary early, Dormition late)

questions.question: From which pages of Shuping’s book does Ortlund read prayers about not daring to approach Christ except through Mary?
In Shuping’s reading, what was Cyprian of Carthage mainly contesting?
options: Rigorists who would not allow the lapsed to return, creating a de facto pure church—not the same as later imperial fine-grained schisms
The authority of the Book of Concord
questions.answer: Rigorists who would not allow the lapsed to return, creating a de facto pure church—not the same as later imperial fine-grained schisms

questions.question: Whose work does Shuping engage in an appendix about unfair comparisons between traditions?
Which Protestant dogmaticians does Shuping recommend for seeing a fuller systematic treatment of soteriology?
options: Thomas Aquinas and Robert Bellarmine only

questions.question: What phrase does Shuping attribute to Kallistos Ware as a way some distance harsh past Orthodox statements?
What analogy does Ortlund use to critique the latria/dulia distinction as a post-hoc justification?
options: Building Noah’s ark with the wrong number of boards
A wife who forbids kissing other women, and a husband later inventing “types of kisses” she never permitted
A bishop moving between Princeton and Westminster
questions.answer: A wife who forbids kissing other women, and a husband later inventing “types of kisses” she never permitted

questions.question: When was this interview published (date given in the transcript header)?
